positions.spread_description: Defence vs IT — long defence basket, short IT basket. Phase-C hedge for geopolitical risk-off regimes.

INFY.thesis: Infosys's consistent achievement of carbon neutrality for 6 years in a row (as of FY2024-25 AR) and the significant progress in increasing green certified space (29.7 million sq.ft. by FY2024-25, well ahead of pro-rata for the 2030 target) demonstrate a strong and verifiable commitment to environmental sustainability.

WIPRO.thesis: **Operating margin resilience and cash generation durability.** Despite a flat full-year net profit growth of 0.36% YoY in FY26 (Mar 2026: 13,266 Cr vs Mar 2025: 13,218 Cr), Wipro has expanded operating margins from 20.2% in Mar 2025 to 19.2% in Mar 2026 (per Screener), and delivered 17.6% operating margin in Q3 FY26 at the high end of the 17-17.5% guided band. This is achieved despite HARMAN DTS integration (which management said would be 'margin-dilutive') and restructuring charges of INR 263 Cr in Q3. **Operating cash flow remained robust at 135% of net income in Q3 and 127.7% for full year FY26** (16,943 Cr OCF vs 13,266 Cr net profit), enabling the company to distribute >$1.3B in dividends (INR 11 per share equivalent) in FY26 while maintaining a gross cash position of $6.5B. This demonstrates **operational discipline and financial stability** even amid demand headwinds. The company is not sacrificing profitability or financial flexibility to chase revenue growth—a disciplined stance that protects shareholder interests.

HAL.thesis: Atmanirbhar Bharat + rising defence budget. But verify production capacity vs order book.

BDL.thesis: BDL dramatically exceeded its GeM (Government e-Marketplace) procurement target, achieving ₹5,841.7 Crore in GMV procurement against a ₹1,602 Crore target in FY2023-24—a 364.6% achievement rate. This demonstrates exceptional execution capability in government procurement processes and strong vendor ecosystem engagement. Additionally, the company exceeded its MSME procurement mandate, achieving 27.70% (₹450.38 Crore) against a 25% statutory target, signalling both policy compliance and supply chain resilience (FY2023-24 AR, Directors' Report - Procurement from MSMEs, Page 16). These routine targets are delivered consistently, indicating reliable operational management at the tactical level.

TCS.thesis: TCS has significantly exceeded its critical environmental target of reducing Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions by 70% by 2025, achieving an 80% reduction (vs 2016 baseline) by FY2024. This demonstrates strong commitment and effective execution on sustainability goals, which are increasingly important for long-term value creation and stakeholder trust.

BEL.thesis: Atmanirbhar Bharat + rising defence budget. But verify production capacity vs order book.

TECHM.thesis: The company's commitment to shareholder returns is a significant strength, as evidenced by the '85% or more of free cash flow generated over five years to shareholders' guidance (FY23-24 AR). Actual dividend payout percentages (94% in FY25, 150% in FY24) and Free Cash Flow to PAT ratios (161.5% in FY25, 123% YTD in Q3 FY26) consistently exceed this target, demonstrating strong capital allocation and shareholder value creation.

CROMPTON.thesis: New category incubation and market entry execution is exceptional. Appliances segment grew 99% in Q1 FY22 from a near-zero base in FY21, with specific sub-category traction (geysers +205%, air coolers +90%, mixer grinders +125% in Q1 FY21; irons +55% in Q1 FY22). By FY24-25, appliance and LDA categories are now material contributors with #5 water heaters and #6 air coolers market rankings (AR). Rural channel delivered 195% growth in Q1 FY22, and rural contribution increased from 48% (fans reach) to 50%+ in rolling 12 months. E-commerce grew 76% in FY24, 149% in Q1 FY22. These expansions are backed by quantified concall evidence and AR actuals, not vague guidance. Premium premiumization strategy delivered: premium/deco fans grew 122%, super-premium fans 258% in Q1 FY22. This demonstrates management's ability to identify and execute market whitespace before it becomes obvious to Street.

MOTILALOFS.thesis: Exceptional execution on CRITICAL profitability and AUM growth metrics. In FY2024-25, consolidated ROE delivered 25% (vs 20%+ target) with PAT of ₹2,508 Cr (+2.5% YoY). AMC AUM surged 72% YoY to ₹1,23,397 Cr, HFC disbursements grew 76% YoY to ₹1,794 Cr (with GNPA/NNPA at 0.8%/0.4% within targets), and distribution AUM expanded 33% YoY to ₹31,551 Cr. Revenue growth of 18.4% (₹8,417 Cr) with annuity revenue >60% of total demonstrates successful business model diversification. The company has also consistently beaten guidance on retail client acquisition (48 Lakh, +14% YoY in FY25) and market share metrics (cash 7.6%, FO 8.5%), proving operational execution excellence.

TATAELXSI.thesis: **Automotive/Transportation vertical has achieved demonstrable ramp-up of large SDV-led deals with sustainable momentum.** Transportation revenue grew 13.8% YoY in 9M FY26 (Rs 1,961.9 Cr) and accelerated 7.7% QoQ in Q3, driven by: (1) successful execution on 'large deals won earlier in the year', (2) 'normalization of work streams' with strategic OEM client that recovered from prior quarter disruption, (3) entry into 'adjacency businesses' (off-road, construction equipment, railway) with 7.5-8% current mix targeting 20% over 2-3 years. This vertical now represents 55%+ of revenue and is delivering both volume growth and geographic/customer diversification (Europe, U.S., Japan, India all showing growth per concall). The segment is also validated by management's credible track record: CEO states 'our share in the overall spend of this customer has only increased' over 5+ years, indicating durable value capture. Operating leverage from transportation growth (200 bps margin benefit in Q3) is concrete and replicable, unlike Media/Healthcare recovery promises which remain unverified.

SONACOMS.thesis: The company's biggest strength lies in its consistent and strong performance in increasing BEV revenue share. The guidance from FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 targeted 'over 45% of revenue from BEVs by FY2025-26' and '45% of revenue from EV-targeted products by 2026'. As of Q1 FY25, BEV revenue share reached 33%, which is ahead of the pro-rata trajectory needed to hit the FY2025-26 target (33% actual vs ~30.33% pro-rata). This demonstrates strong execution on a critical strategic priority.
